description: Eleven real voluntary carbon market projects used as demo cases. synthetic_forest_bias_before/after control offline fallback so the demo tells the right story without network. When real Planetary Computer data is available, these fields are ignored.

projects.name: Kariba REDD+
summary_claims: We are proud to deliver a world-class REDD+ project that protects forests and empowers communities.
Our carbon credits represent high-integrity, high-impact climate action.
The Kariba project has retained substantial forest cover across the project area.
Community benefits agreements signed with local stewards totaling 785,000 hectares of protected forest.
This project is fully verified under VCS and delivers genuine, additional climate benefit.
projects.context: Center of 2023 controversy after investigations questioned baseline deforestation assumptions. Canonical test case for auditor systems.

projects.name: Alto Mayo Conservation Initiative
summary_claims: Alto Mayo Protected Forest covers 182,000 hectares of tropical montane forest.
Baseline deforestation rate estimated at 1.2% annually pre-project.
Project area covers 182,000 hectares of primary cloud forest.
VM0015 methodology applied with 20% buffer contribution.
Field patrols operate across 12 patrol routes totaling 428 km.
projects.context: One of the largest REDD+ projects in the Amazon; generally regarded as well-documented.

projects.name: Cordillera Azul National Park REDD+
summary_claims: Cordillera Azul spans 1.35 million hectares of Amazonian forest.
Total credits issued to date: 29 million VCUs over the crediting period.
Monitoring plots: 138 permanent sample plots of 0.1 hectare each.
Project is committed to world-class forest protection and community uplift.
Leakage estimated at 10% using VT0001 tool and discounted from net credits.
projects.context: Large-scale project; mix of specific and vague language in PDD.

projects.name: Southern Cardamom REDD+
summary_claims: Our project protects 497,000 hectares of rainforest in the Cardamom Mountains.
We deliver genuine and lasting climate impact through community-led conservation.
Our safeguards are best in class.
Community benefits reached over 16,000 residents across 29 villages.
Annual monitoring reports filed with Verra since 2017.
projects.context: High-profile project with ongoing scrutiny over community consent and outcomes.

projects.name: Kasigau Corridor REDD+
summary_claims: Project area: 500,000 hectares of Acacia-Commiphora dry forest between Tsavo East and West parks.
Baseline deforestation rate: 0.6% per year (2000-2010) using Hansen Global Forest Change data.
302 permanent employees recruited from 6 local communities; census-verified headcount June 2022.
Annual monitoring by Wildlife Works Carbon using Landsat 8 cloud-masked composites at 30m resolution.
Total VCUs issued 2011-2022: 3,184,000; retired by corporates: 2,071,400.
projects.context: Widely regarded as one of Africa's most transparent and legitimate REDD+ projects. Often cited as a model by UNFCCC.

projects.name: Mai Ndombe REDD+
summary_claims: Mai Ndombe represents transformative climate leadership in the Congo Basin.
Our project delivers significant environmental and social co-benefits.
We are committed to the highest standards of forest protection.
Credits issued represent genuine, additional climate action.
The project area has retained substantial forest cover under our stewardship.
projects.context: One of the world's largest REDD+ projects (~3M ha). Came under scrutiny in 2023 over baseline inflation and community displacement allegations.

projects.name: Surui Forest Carbon Project
summary_claims: The Surui indigenous territory covers 248,147 hectares of Amazon rainforest.
Baseline deforestation was 1.47% per year from 2001-2009 (INPE PRODES data).
Forest patrols carried out by 58 Surui warriors trained in GPS and field data collection.
Above-ground carbon stock: 168 tC/ha measured via 96 allometric sampling plots in 2010.
Net emission reductions verified by SGS S.A. for 2010-2012 period: 996,000 tCO2e.
projects.context: Pioneering indigenous-led REDD+ project. Suspended in 2018 due to illegal logging incursions; partially reinstated. Mixed record of credibility.

projects.name: Rimba Raya Biodiversity Reserve
summary_claims: Rimba Raya protects 64,531 hectares of peat swamp forest in Central Kalimantan.
Baseline scenario: conversion to oil palm plantation at 100% probability per provincial permit.
Peat depth measured at 214 sample points; mean depth 3.4 m across project area.
Annual fire monitoring using MODIS active fire alerts; 6 suppression events in 2022.
Biodiversity co-benefits: 36 Bornean orangutan nest sites confirmed in 2021 survey.
projects.context: Frequently cited as a technically rigorous project. Delayed for years by Indonesian government permit disputes. Generally medium-high integrity.

projects.name: Ntakata Forest REDD+
summary_claims: Ntakata Forest covers 386,000 hectares of Miombo woodland near Katavi National Park.
Historical deforestation: 1.9% per year (2010-2016) validated using GFC Hansen v1.6.
We are committed to protecting nature and empowering local communities.
200 village boundary markers GPS-referenced and filed with district land office.
Monitoring uses annual Landsat 8 composites processed in Google Earth Engine.
projects.context: Mid-sized Tanzanian project with mixed track record. Claimed deforestation baseline is higher than nearby reference regions, raising additionality concerns.

projects.name: CAR REDD+ Klamath East
summary_claims: Project area: 48,210 acres of mixed conifer forest in Siskiyou County, California.
Enrolled under California Air Resources Board (CARB) Compliance Offset Protocol for U.S. Forest Projects.
100-year timber harvest schedule deferred; verified harvest volumes: 0 MMBF in 2020-2022 period.
Carbon stocks verified by South Pole Group using USFS FIA allometric equations.
Total ARB offset credits issued: 187,432; all retired by California compliance entities.
projects.context: Regulated under California cap-and-trade. Strong oversight by CARB with third-party verification. One of the more credible project types globally.

projects.name: Demo: Healthy Reforestation Site
summary_claims: Baseline above-ground biomass: 78 tC per hectare as measured via LiDAR in 2016.
Between 2016 and 2023, canopy cover increased from 68% to 84% per Sentinel-2 analysis.
Total area planted: 4,120 hectares of native species across 3 contiguous parcels.
Validation conducted by a third-party auditor in October 2022.
Net GHG removals in 2022: 38,240 tCO2e after buffer contribution.
projects.context: Synthetic 'good project' control case — increasing forest cover, specific numbers, third-party verified. Designed to contrast with Kariba.
